sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: This Stock Is Already Up 58% This Year. Is It a Buy?
feed.summary: Moderna's stock is up 58% this year due to promising clinical trial results for its drug candidate, intismeran autogene, which, in combination with Merck's Keytruda, showed a 49% reduction in disease recurrence or death in advanced melanoma patients over five years. This success, along with its innovative mRNA platform and other candidates in clinical trials, suggests a potential for sustained growth despite past revenue challenges. The article questions if this marks the beginning of a sustained bull run for Moderna, especially given its significant drop in share price over the last three years.

feed.title: Analysts Offer Insights on Energy Companies: Baker Hughes Company (BKR) and Delek US Holdings (DK)
feed.summary: BMO Capital maintained a Buy rating on Baker Hughes Company (BKR) with a $65.00 price target, while Morgan Stanley maintained a Hold rating on Delek US Holdings (DK) with a $38.00 price target. Analyst consensus for BKR is a Strong Buy with a 5.2% upside, and for DK is a Hold with a 43.0% upside. The article highlights recent analyst reports and price targets for these energy sector companies.

feed.title: Spinal Cord Injury Market: Growth Momentum Across 7MM to 2034 – DelveInsight | Kringle Pharma, Neuroplast, Abbvie, Mitsubishi Tanabe Pharma, Lineage Cell Therapeutics, NervGen Pharma
feed.summary: The Spinal Cord Injury (SCI) market is anticipated to grow significantly across the 7 Major Markets (7MM) from $354 million in 2025 to $1,456 million by 2034, driven by increasing prevalence and advancements in therapeutic development. Key companies including Kringle Pharma, Neuroplast, Abbvie, and others are actively engaged in developing novel therapies, including allogeneic stem cell treatments and neurotechnologies. The report highlights historical and forecasted epidemiology, market trends, drug uptake, and the current treatment landscape for SCI.

feed.title: Reeflex Solutions Inc. Provides Bi-weekly Status Update on MCTO
feed.summary: Reeflex Solutions Inc. announced a bi-weekly status update on its Management Cease Trade Order (MCTO) due to delays in filing annual financial statements and related certifications for the financial year ended August 31, 2025. The delay stems from a complex transaction transitioning to a public company and the need to restate Q3 filings to comply with IFRS. The company expects to complete the outstanding filings, including Q1 filings, by February 19, 2026, and is actively working to resolve the issues.

feed.title: CPNG DEADLINE NOTICE: Coupang, Inc. Investors Encouraged to Contact Kirby McInerney LLP By February 17, 2026
feed.summary: Kirby McInerney LLP is encouraging investors who suffered losses in Coupang, Inc. (NYSE:CPNG) to contact the firm regarding a securities fraud class action lawsuit. The lawsuit alleges that Coupang made false statements and failed to disclose inadequate cybersecurity protocols that led to a significant data breach, impacting its stock price on multiple occasions. Investors have until February 17, 2026, to apply to be appointed as lead plaintiff.

feed.title: Western Alliance Bancorporation (NYSE:WAL) Price Target Raised to $110.00
feed.summary: Jefferies Financial Group has raised its price target for Western Alliance Bancorporation (NYSE:WAL) to $110.00 from $105.00, maintaining a "buy" rating, indicating a potential upside of approximately 24%. This follows strong Q4 earnings, where the company beat EPS and revenue expectations. Other analysts have also adjusted their targets, contributing to a consensus "Moderate Buy" rating for WAL.

feed.title: Colony Bankcorp: Q4 Earnings Snapshot
feed.summary: Colony Bankcorp Inc. reported net income of $7.8 million for its fourth quarter, translating to 42 cents per share, or 48 cents adjusted for merger costs. The bank holding company posted revenue of $21.5 million and net interest expense of $13.9 million, surpassing Street forecasts. For the full year, the company's profit was $28.3 million, or $1.52 per share, on revenue of $102.2 million.

feed.title: Honda’s 2025 New Vehicle Sales Top Previous Year’s Numbers
feed.summary: American Honda reported a slight increase in new vehicle sales for 2025, reaching 1.43 million units despite supplier shortages, a 0.5 percent rise year-over-year. This growth was primarily driven by a 2.6 percent jump in truck sales across both Honda and Acura brands, which offset declines in car sales. Key performers included the CR-V, the redesigned Passport with a significant sales increase, and the all-electric Acura ZDX and Honda Prologue.

feed.title: Wash Cycle: Driven Brands sells its car wash business
feed.summary: Driven Brands Holdings Inc. has completed the sale of its international car wash business, IMO Car Wash, to Franchise Equity Partners for approximately US$492 million. This strategic move allows Driven Brands to focus on its core automotive services and reduce debt. Franchise Equity Partners plans to optimize IMO Car Wash's operations and expand its international multi-unit retail expertise.

feed.title: STERIS Announces Dividend of $0.63 per share
feed.summary: STERIS plc (NYSE: STE) announced a quarterly dividend of $0.63 per share, payable on March 19, 2026, to shareholders of record as of February 17, 2026. The company is a global provider of products and services focusing on infection prevention and supporting patient care. The announcement also included a cautionary statement regarding forward-looking information, outlining potential risks that could affect future results.

feed.title: Top performing healthcare stocks in the past month (MRNA:NASDAQ)
feed.summary: This article identifies the top 10 best-performing healthcare stocks with market capitalizations of $10 billion or more based on their one-month price performance as January 2026 concludes. It notes that earnings season is accelerating, putting these stocks in focus. The list serves as a snapshot of strong performers in the healthcare sector for the given period.

feed.title: Gilead Oncology Momentum Builds As Trodelvy Data Meet Strong Share Gains
feed.summary: Gilead Sciences' Trodelvy, combined with Keytruda, demonstrated positive Phase 3 results for first-line metastatic triple-negative breast cancer, showing improved progression-free survival and favorable safety. This news contributes to Gilead's strong recent share performance and bolsters its oncology ambitions. The company has filed for expanded regulatory approval of Trodelvy based on these findings.

feed.title: Gabelli Mario J buys Gabelli Dividend (GDV) shares worth $157945 By Investing.com
feed.summary: Gabelli Mario J, through GGCP, Inc., purchased 5,500 shares of Gabelli Dividend & Income Trust (GDV) for $157,945 on January 27, 2026. This transaction increased his indirect ownership to 20,500 shares, in addition to his direct and other indirect holdings. GDV maintains a "GOOD" financial health rating with a 6.28% dividend yield and a 23-year history of dividend payments.

feed.title: Amcor PLC (NYSE:AMCR) Short Interest Down 74.0% in January
feed.summary: Amcor PLC (NYSE:AMCR) experienced a significant 74.0% decrease in short interest during January, with total short interest falling to 25,866,081 shares as of January 15th. This reduction correlates with the company's strong stock performance, recent dividend increase to $0.65 per share (a 5.9% yield), and positive analyst ratings, leading to an average price target of $52.14. Shares are trading at $44.19, reflecting a P/E ratio of 12.25 and a market capitalization of $20.37 billion.

feed.title: Amgen Faces 340B Legal Challenge As Stock Trades Near Analyst Views
feed.summary: Amgen is facing a lawsuit from Sagebrush Health Services concerning allegations of improper actions related to the 340B Drug Pricing Program, including cutting off discounted drug sales and retracting previously granted discounts. The legal challenge introduces potential reputational and financial risks for Amgen, particularly regarding how it interacts with safety net providers, while its stock currently trades near analyst targets and shows positive momentum. Investors are advised to monitor the progression of the 340B case, any regulatory responses, and Amgen's disclosures regarding its 340B policies.

feed.title: Phillips 66 (PSX) Advances While Market Declines: Some Information for Investors
feed.summary: Phillips 66 (PSX) saw its stock price increase by 1.48% to $142.08 in the latest trading session, outperforming the S&P 500, which experienced a slight decline. The company is set to release its earnings on February 4, 2026, with EPS projected to rise by 1500% year-over-year. Currently, PSX holds a Zacks Rank of #3 (Hold) and is trading at a Forward P/E ratio of 12.34, a discount compared to its industry's average of 13.4.

feed.title: The Truth About Electronic Arts Stock: Hidden Power Play or Overhyped GG?
feed.summary: This article analyzes whether Electronic Arts (EA) stock is a smart investment or overhyped, examining its market performance, social media presence, business models, and competition. It delves into EA's reliance on live services, sports franchises, and its extensive IP library, concluding that EA represents a stable, mid-risk option for long-term investors seeking gaming industry exposure.

feed.title: Viavi Solutions (NASDAQ:VIAV) Surprises With Q4 CY2025 Sales
feed.summary: Viavi Solutions (NASDAQ:VIAV) exceeded Q4 CY2025 revenue expectations, with sales increasing 36.4% year-on-year to $369.3 million, and provided surprisingly positive revenue guidance for Q1 CY2026. The company reported non-GAAP profit of $0.22 per share, beating analyst estimates by 17.2%. Despite strong revenue growth, its operating margin declined, and adjusted EBITDA missed expectations.

feed.title: Donanemab by Eli Lilly and Company expected to drive market size of Alzheimer's Disease
feed.summary: A new report by DelveInsight forecasts that Eli Lilly and Company's Donanemab will significantly drive the Alzheimer's Disease market size, with detailed sales projections up to 2034. The growth is attributed to its potential as a disease-modifying therapy for early symptomatic Alzheimer's, increasing patient initiations, physician confidence, and targeted commercial strategies by Eli Lilly. The report covers market assessments, clinical overviews, and competitive landscapes across major global markets.

feed.title: PG&E (PCG) Analyst Ratings Update: Jefferies Lowers Price Target | PCG Stock News
feed.summary: Jefferies has maintained its "Buy" rating on PG&E (PCG) but lowered the price target from $21.00 to $20.00, representing a 4.76% decrease. This adjustment reflects an updated assessment of the company's market performance and future outlook. Despite the lowered target, the average analyst price target for PCG is $21.27, suggesting a 42.54% upside from its current price, with an overall "Outperform" recommendation from brokerage firms.

feed.title: Pfizer's Revenue Rebound Potential is Significant
feed.summary: Pfizer is facing a projected revenue decline in 2025 as it struggles to recover from record COVID-19 vaccine sales and diversify its drug pipeline. However, the company has significant growth potential through new oncology drugs expected by 2030 and its entry into the weight-loss market via the Metsera acquisition. Recent acquisitions like Arena Pharmaceuticals and Seagen further strengthen its future growth prospects, though many new drugs will not complete testing until after 2030.

feed.title: Goldman Sachs Downgrades Indonesian Stocks After MSCI Warning
feed.summary: Goldman Sachs has downgraded Indonesian stocks to underweight following a warning from MSCI regarding the market's investability. The Wall Street bank estimates that a downgrade to frontier status by MSCI could trigger over $13 billion in outflows, with passive funds tracking MSCI indexes selling up to $7.8 billion and a further $5.6 billion if FTSE Russell also reassesses its methodology. This poses a significant risk to the Indonesian market.

feed.title: C.H. Robinson Worldwide Q4 Earnings Call Highlights
feed.summary: C.H. Robinson Worldwide (NASDAQ: CHRW) successfully navigated a challenging Q4, marked by weak freight demand and declining ocean rates, through strategic focus on service, pricing discipline, and productivity. The company achieved market share gains in North American Surface Transportation (NAST) and improved margins, largely driven by its "Lean AI" operating model which delivered significant productivity improvements and cost reductions. Despite pressures in Global Forwarding, the company generated substantial cash flow and returned significant capital to shareholders, while maintaining strong liquidity.

feed.title: CenterPoint Energy Inc.: How a Traditional Utility Is Rewiring Itself for the Grid of the 2030s
feed.summary: CenterPoint Energy Inc. is transforming from a traditional gas-and-power utility into a data-driven grid platform, focusing on smart meters, resilience, and electrification for its future. The company is investing in advanced infrastructure, data-driven operations, and support for distributed energy resources, and modernizing its natural gas pipelines. CenterPoint differentiates itself by being a resilient, software-defined network in high-growth, weather-exposed markets, aiming for stable earnings growth and customer satisfaction.

feed.title: VICI Properties Inc. (VICI) Falls More Steeply Than Broader Market: What Investors Need to Know
feed.summary: VICI Properties Inc. (VICI) closed down 2.14% in its latest trading session, underperforming the broader S&P 500, but has seen a 0.56% gain over the last month. The company is set to release its earnings on February 25, 2026, with projected earnings per share of $0.6 and revenue of $1.02 billion. VICI currently holds a Zacks Rank of #3 (Hold) and its valuation metrics, such as a Forward P/E of 11.65, indicate a slight premium compared to its industry average.

feed.title: JPMorgan Chase & Co. Reduces Stake in Yum Brands Inc.
feed.summary: JPMorgan Chase & Co. has significantly reduced its stake in Yum Brands Inc. by 11,804,341 shares, a 37.05% reduction, with the shares traded at $151.28. Despite this strategic portfolio adjustment, Yum Brands remains a notable holding for JPMorgan Chase & Co. The article highlights Yum Brands' strong financial metrics, including a high GF Score and profitability rank, and mentions other significant investors like Joel Greenblatt and Ken Fisher.

feed.title: Invesco’s 2025 Recapitalization And China JV Reshape Earnings Outlook
feed.summary: In 2025, Invesco recapitalized its balance sheet and significantly grew its China joint venture, marking a transformative phase for the global asset manager. These developments aim to free up more earnings for common shareholders and broaden Invesco's international footprint, particularly in the growing wealth management market in China. While the company reported a net loss for the year, these strategic moves are expected to influence Invesco's future earnings profile and geographic mix.

feed.title: Boston Properties (BXP) Steady FFO Highlights Cash Focus And Challenges Bearish Narratives
feed.summary: Boston Properties (BXP) reported Q4 FY 2025 revenue of US$923.4 million and FFO of US$280.2 million, showcasing a focus on cash generation despite swings in basic EPS. While FFO appears more stable than EPS and the stock trades below fair value estimates, concerns remain regarding weak interest coverage and a high P/E ratio relative to peers. Investors are urged to consider long-term trends and balance sheet strength against these mixed signals.

feed.title: C.H. Robinson Worldwide Q4 Earnings Call Highlights
feed.summary: C.H. Robinson Worldwide (CHRW) navigated a challenging Q4 marked by weak global freight demand and lower ocean rates, but achieved market share gains and improved margins in North American Surface Transportation (NAST). The company emphasized its "Lean AI" operating model, delivering significant productivity gains and cost reductions, and reiterated its 2026 expense and capital expenditure guidance. Despite headwinds, CHRW generated substantial cash from operations and returned capital to shareholders.

feed.title: Badger Meter (NYSE:BMI) Misses Q4 CY2025 Revenue Estimates, Stock Drops
feed.summary: Badger Meter (NYSE:BMI) reported Q4 CY2025 results, missing Wall Street's revenue expectations with $220.7 million, despite a 7.6% year-on-year sales increase. However, the company beat GAAP EPS estimates by 1.5%, reporting $1.14 per share, causing the stock to drop 6.9% immediately after the announcement. The company also announced executive leadership changes effective January 1, 2026, aimed at aligning strategic priorities with its management structure to expand market leadership in cellular Advanced Metering Infrastructure (AMI).

feed.title: Did HPE’s (HPE) 2degrees Private AI Cloud Deal Just Recast Its Telecom Infrastructure Strategy?
feed.summary: Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) has partnered with New Zealand telecom 2degrees to implement a private AI cloud platform, leveraging HPE Private Cloud AI and NVIDIA infrastructure to enhance network reliability, security, and data sovereignty. This deal aligns with HPE's strategy to pivot towards recurring, high-value cloud and AI infrastructure, especially in the telecom sector, though its immediate impact on revenue and margins is expected to be modest. While HPE faces challenges with unprofitability and debt, its focus on telecom-grade AI infrastructure is a key part of its long-term investment narrative.

feed.title: Assessing Microchip Technology (MCHP) Valuation After maXTouch M1 Automotive Touchscreen Expansion
feed.summary: Microchip Technology (MCHP) is gaining attention after expanding its maXTouch M1 touchscreen controller family for automotive displays. Despite recent strong stock performance and trading near analyst targets, the company is considered approximately 6.9% overvalued with a fair value of $75.12. The valuation is based on expectations of faster revenue growth, higher margins, and a rich future earnings multiple across recovering end-markets, though risks like elevated inventory and substantial debt remain.

feed.title: Assurant, Inc. (AIZ) Announces Board Appointment and Resignation
feed.summary: Assurant, Inc. (AIZ) has announced changes to its board of directors, appointing Lynn Blake and accepting the resignation of Debra Perry. Lynn Blake, formerly of State Street Investment Management, brings extensive experience and will join key committees. Debra Perry's resignation is in line with the company's director retirement policy.

feed.title: BPY-UN.TO C$23.29 Brookfield Property Partners TSX Jan 28 2026: 15.50M shares traded
feed.summary: Brookfield Property Partners (BPY-UN.TO) closed at C$23.29 on January 28, 2026, with an extraordinary trading volume of 15.5 million shares, significantly higher than its average. This surge in activity, indicative of block trades or renewed investor interest in its diversified real estate portfolio, positions the stock among the most active on the TSX. Despite negative reported EPS, the company shows strong book values, and Meyka AI rates it a 'HOLD' with a base case target of C$26.00 and a bear case of C$18.00.

feed.title: Salesforce launches MuleSoft Agent Fabric to rein in corporate AI sprawl - CHOSUNBIZ
feed.summary: Salesforce has launched MuleSoft Agent Fabric, a new offering designed to help companies manage and integrate their growing number of AI agents. The platform aims to prevent AI sprawl by providing a unified way to connect, orchestrate, and secure AI deployments across an organization. This initiative reflects the increasing need for robust management solutions as businesses adopt more AI technologies.

feed.title: Tariff war cost Canadian Pacific $200M over past year: CEO
feed.summary: Canadian Pacific Kansas City Ltd. (CPKC) CEO Keith Creel stated that the ongoing tariff war initiated by the United States has cost the company $200 million over the past year. Despite this, he remained optimistic about the renegotiation of the United States-Mexico-Canada Agreement (USMCA), emphasizing its critical importance for trade among the three nations. Creel also expressed strong opposition to a proposed US$85-billion merger between Union Pacific Corp. and Norfolk Southern Corp., warning of potential damage to competition and the North American rail transportation system.

feed.title: Motorola Solutions to Issue Fourth-Quarter and Full-Year 2025 Earnings Results on February 11
feed.summary: Motorola Solutions (NYSE: MSI) will release its fourth-quarter and full-year 2025 earnings results after the market closes on Wednesday, February 11. The company will also host a conference call for financial analysts at 4 p.m. Central (5 p.m. Eastern) on the same day. A live webcast of the conference call will be available on the Motorola Solutions investor relations website.

feed.title: Tired of Super Bowl spoilers? Xfinity’s new 4K stream cuts the delay
feed.summary: Xfinity has launched RealTime4K, a new ultra-low latency technology that provides the fastest and most immersive 4K viewing experience for live sports, including the Super Bowl. This technology reduces delay by up to 30 seconds compared to other 4K providers and incorporates Dolby Vision and Dolby Atmos for superior audio and video. Xfinity TV customers can access this through a new Peacock 4K channel, offering a full day of 4K Olympics and Super Bowl coverage.

feed.title: Scotiabank Boosts B2Gold Price Target Inspiring Investor Optimism
feed.summary: Scotiabank has increased its price target for B2Gold from C$8 to C$10, maintaining a Sector Perform rating, while Raymond James also raised its target from $6 to $6.50 with an Outperform rating. These adjustments reflect potential growth spending and increased investor confidence, leading to a 4.22% rise in B2Gold Corp (Canada) stock. The company's robust revenues, decent EBIT margin, and solid current ratio underpin a promising outlook, with market reactions signaling trust in its current trajectory.

feed.title: Jersey Mike's is reportedly planning an IPO, just one year after its sale to Blackstone
feed.summary: Jersey Mike's is reportedly planning an IPO as soon as the third quarter of this year, just one year after its sale to Blackstone. The sandwich chain has hired Morgan Stanley and JPMorgan Chase as advisors and could be valued at up to $12 billion. This move follows Blackstone's acquisition of Jersey Mike's for $8 billion and the appointment of Charlie Morrison, who previously took Wingstop public, as CEO.

feed.title: Kid Rock Slams Ticketing Industry for Unfair Practices in Testimony
feed.summary: Kid Rock testified before the Senate Commerce Committee, lambasting the ticketing industry for unfair practices that harm artists and fans. He highlighted the failure of the Live Nation and Ticketmaster merger to deliver on promises of lower costs and increased competition, pushing for congressional intervention to address what he described as widespread fraud and abuse. Rock suggested solutions such as artists controlling ticket sales, price caps on resales, and outlawing speculative ticketing.

feed.title: Costco lied about rotisserie chicken being 'preservative free,' lawsuit says
feed.summary: A class-action lawsuit has been filed against Costco Wholesale, alleging that the company falsely advertised its Kirkland Signature rotisserie chicken as "preservative-free." The lawsuit claims that the chicken, in fact, contains sodium phosphate and carrageenan. Costco has stated that it removed "preservative-free" statements from its labeling to maintain consistency and that these ingredients are approved by food safety authorities for moisture retention and texture.

feed.title: Atkore declares quarterly dividend of $0.33 per share
feed.summary: Atkore Inc. (NYSE:ATKR) announced a quarterly cash dividend of $0.33 per share, payable on February 27, 2026, to stockholders of record as of February 17, 2026. The company, which reported $2.9 billion in sales for fiscal year 2025 and has a strong current ratio, will report its next quarterly earnings on February 3, 2026. This dividend declaration follows recent financial results where Atkore missed EPS expectations but exceeded revenue, and it also recently sold its Tectron mechanical tube product line.

feed.title: Alexandria Real Estate Equities Begins $800 Million Tender Offer From Pasadena Headquarters
feed.summary: Alexandria Real Estate Equities, headquartered in Pasadena, has initiated cash tender offers to repurchase up to $800 million of its outstanding senior notes. The offer targets specific series of senior notes due in 2050, 2051, and 2052, with an early tender premium for notes submitted by February 9th. The company's obligation to complete the tender offers is contingent on securing at least $500 million in gross proceeds from other capital markets financing transactions.

feed.title: Packaging Corp of America Highlights Growth Amid Heavy Capex
feed.summary: Packaging Corporation of America (PCA) reported strong Q4 and full-year 2025 results, demonstrating robust top-line growth, improved profitability (despite a quarterly dip in adjusted EPS), and record cash generation, driven by the successful integration of Greif acquisitions and effective pricing strategies. While facing headwinds from heavy capital expenditures, operational costs, and weather disruptions, the company is making strategic investments in energy projects and maintaining a tight containerboard market position, signaling continued growth and structural cost improvements despite near-term earnings pressures.

feed.title: CSX Corporation honors Catherine Adkins for pioneering real estate innovation
feed.summary: CSX Corporation has recognized Catherine Adkins, their Director of Real Estate, for her innovative contributions to modernizing real estate systems and advancing AI technologies over two decades. Adkins, also named to the 2026 Women of Influence in Rail, has been instrumental in positioning CSX as a forward-thinking transportation industry leader. This recognition highlights CSX's commitment to technology and talent, aligning with broader initiatives to enhance operational efficiency and strategic growth within the rail sector.

feed.title: Williams-Sonoma (WSM) Suffers a Larger Drop Than the General Market: Key Insights
feed.summary: Williams-Sonoma (WSM) experienced a 1.21% drop, underperforming the broader S&P 500's slight loss but outperforming the Retail-Wholesale sector and S&P 500 over the past month. The company's upcoming earnings are projected to show a decline in EPS and revenue, though annual estimates suggest slight revenue growth. WSM currently holds a Zacks Rank of #2 (Buy) and its valuation metrics, including a Forward P/E of 23.96, are in line with its industry average.

feed.title: Blue Bird (BLBD) Suffers a Larger Drop Than the General Market: Key Insights
feed.summary: Blue Bird (BLBD) stock closed down 1.56% at $49.82, underperforming the S&P 500. Despite this recent dip, the company's shares have risen 8.63% over the last month, outperforming its sector. Investors are awaiting Blue Bird's upcoming earnings report on February 4, 2026, which is projected to show an EPS drop but a revenue increase.

feed.title: Tariff war cost Canadian Pacific $200M over past year, CEO says
feed.summary: Canadian Pacific Kansas City Ltd. (CPKC) CEO Keith Creel stated that the ongoing tariff war initiated by the United States has cost the company $200 million over the past year. Despite this, Creel remains optimistic about the renegotiation of the United States-Mexico-Canada Agreement (USMCA), emphasizing its importance for all three nations. He also expressed strong opposition to a proposed US$85-billion merger between Union Pacific Corp. and Norfolk Southern Corp., warning it could harm competition and the North American rail transportation system.

feed.title: Noble completes $360-million sale of five jackups to Borr Drilling
feed.summary: Noble Corporation has finalized the sale of five premium jackup drilling rigs to Borr Drilling for $360 million, receiving $210 million in cash and $150 million in seller notes. Noble will continue to operate some of these rigs under bareboat charters for a period. This acquisition expands Borr Drilling's jackup fleet to 29 rigs and aligns with Noble's portfolio management strategy, while strengthening Borr's position in the offshore jackup market.
